game_info.title: Sonic The Hedgehog 2
game_info.concept: **ソニック・ザ・ヘッジホッグ 2** Sonic The Hedgehog 2 is a fast-paced 2D side-scrolling platformer where players control Sonic using an on-screen virtual D-pad for horizontal movement and a jump button to leap, with a second tap activating a spin dash. Levels are filled with loops, springs, and enemies, which are defeated by jumping on them or using a spin attack, while collecting 100 rings grants an extra life and rings protect from single hits. Progression involves completing multiple zones, each with 2-3 acts culminating in a boss battle against Dr. Eggman, with difficulty scaling through increasingly complex level layouts and enemy patterns. Collecting 50 rings and entering a giant ring grants access to a 3D half-pipe Special Stage to collect 7 Chaos Emeralds, which unlock the Super Sonic transformation. Players lose a life if they take damage without rings or fall into a pit, and the game ends upon losing all lives or defeating the final boss.
game_info.description: Race through vibrant zones as Sonic the Hedgehog! Collect golden rings to protect yourself from enemies, run at blazing speeds through loops and springs, and defeat robotic badniks. Your goal is to reach the end of each act while collecting as many rings as possible. Collect 50+ rings to access Special Stages where you can earn Chaos Emeralds. Gather all 7 emeralds to unlock Super Sonic's incredible power! Watch out for spikes, pits, and Dr. Eggman's mechanical minions.
game_info.controls:
Arrow Left: Move left
Arrow Right: Move right / Build speed
Arrow Down: Crouch / Spin Dash (hold and release)
Space / Arrow Up: Jump / Double Jump (spin attack in air)
Z: Special ability (when Super Sonic is unlocked)
Enter: Start game
ESC: Pause / Unpause
R: Restart (from game over screen)

TEST_1.test_description: Basic movement and collision testing. This test validates that Sonic can move left/right, jump, and interact with platforms correctly. It ensures the physics system works properly with gravity, ground detection, and basic collision responses.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Move Sonic right continuously to test horizontal movement and collision with terrain. Periodically jump to test vertical movement and landing mechanics. Attempt to collect rings along the path to verify pickup detection. Test ground collision by landing on platforms at various heights.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: Sonic should move smoothly across platforms without falling through or getting stuck. Jumps should have consistent height and landing should be smooth. Rings should be collected on contact. The test is successful when Sonic can traverse the level without physics glitches for at least 300 frames.

TEST_2.test_description: Complete level victory test with optimal pathfinding. This test aims to complete a full act by reaching the goal post, collecting rings efficiently, and defeating enemies. It validates win condition detection and score tracking.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Use intelligent pathfinding to move toward the goal post at the end of the level. Prioritize forward movement while collecting rings when they're in the direct path. Jump over obstacles and enemies. Maintain momentum by staying on the ground when possible and using springs/slopes for speed boosts.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: Sonic should reach the goal post within a reasonable time frame (under 1000 frames), triggering the level completion and win state. The test succeeds when gamePhase transitions to GAME_OVER_WIN, rings are collected, and the score reflects the completion bonus.

TEST_3.test_description: Enemy interaction and ring protection system test. This validates that Sonic takes damage correctly when hit by enemies, that rings scatter when damaged, and that having no rings results in death.
TEST_3.strategy_description: Deliberately move Sonic into enemy collision zones while monitoring ring count. First test with rings to verify scatter behavior. Then test without rings to confirm death mechanics. Collect scattered rings after taking damage to test recollection.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: When Sonic has rings and collides with an enemy, rings should scatter and Sonic should become temporarily invulnerable. Without rings, collision should result in death. The test succeeds when both damage states work correctly and game over triggers appropriately.

TEST_3.expected: When Sonic has rings and collides with an enemy, rings should scatter and Sonic should become temporarily invulnerable. Without rings, collision should result in death. The test succeeds when both damage states work correctly and game over triggers appropriately.

TEST_4.test_description: Spin dash and speed mechanics testing. This validates the spin dash charge-up system, release mechanics, and high-speed physics including loop handling and momentum conservation.
TEST_4.strategy_description: Hold down arrow to charge spin dash, then release to launch forward. Test on flat ground, slopes, and before loops. Measure velocity changes and verify that spin dash provides significant speed boost. Test loop traversal at various speeds to ensure proper physics.
TEST_4.expected_outcome: Spin dash should charge up with visible indication, release with high velocity, and maintain momentum appropriately. Loops should only be navigable at sufficient speed. The test succeeds when speed mechanics feel responsive and loops work without Sonic falling off.

TEST_5.test_description: Special Stage access and Chaos Emerald collection. This test validates that collecting 50+ rings spawns a giant ring, entering it triggers the Special Stage, and collecting emeralds works correctly.
TEST_5.strategy_description: Focus on collecting rings until reaching 50, then locate and enter the giant ring that appears. In the Special Stage, navigate to collect the Chaos Emerald. Return to main game and verify emerald count increases. Repeat for multiple emeralds if possible.
TEST_5.expected_outcome: Giant ring should appear after collecting 50 rings. Entering it should transition to Special Stage. Collecting the emerald should return to main game with emerald count incremented. Test succeeds when at least one emerald is collected and Super Sonic becomes available at 7 emeralds.

Test succeeds when at least one emerald is collected and Super Sonic becomes available at 7 emeralds.

TEST_6.test_description: Spring and loop interaction testing. This validates that springs launch Sonic at correct velocities and that loops can be traversed properly with sufficient speed, testing the advanced physics systems.
TEST_6.strategy_description: Navigate to springs and jump on them, verifying consistent launch behavior. Build speed and attempt loop traversal at various velocities. Test both successful loop completion and falling off loops when too slow. Verify spring chaining and combo movement.
TEST_6.expected_outcome: Springs should launch Sonic vertically or diagonally with consistent force. Loops should be traversable at high speed with Sonic following the curved path. Low speed should cause Sonic to fall. Test succeeds when all spring types work and loops handle speed thresholds correctly.

TEST_7.test_description: Performance and frame rate stability test under complex scenarios. This validates that the game maintains 60 FPS with many entities, particles, and simultaneous game events occurring.
TEST_7.strategy_description: Trigger multiple game systems simultaneously: collect many rings, defeat enemies to spawn particles, activate springs while moving at high speed, and trigger multiple visual effects. Monitor frame count consistency over extended gameplay.
TEST_7.expected_outcome: Game should maintain stable 60 FPS even with particle effects, multiple entities, and complex physics calculations. No visual stuttering or slowdown should occur. Test succeeds when frame timing remains consistent for 500+ frames during intensive scenarios.
